subject: Social Science (History)
chapter_name: The Making of the National Movement: 1870s–1947
overall_learningObjectives: Explain the rise of political consciousness in India during the late nineteenth century
Understand the formation and objectives of the Indian National Congress
Analyze the role of moderate and extremist leaders in the freedom movement
Identify major national movements such as Non-Cooperation, Civil Disobedience and Quit India
Understand the participation of peasants, workers, women and students in the national movement
Explain the influence of nationalist ideas through newspapers and political organizations
Understand the key events that led to India’s independence in 1947

lesson_plan.title: Rise of Nationalism in India
topics_covered: Growth of political awareness in the late 19th century
Impact of colonial rule on Indian society
student_activities: Identify reasons for the growth of nationalism
Discuss the impact of British policies on Indians

lesson_plan.title: Formation of the Indian National Congress
topics_covered: Establishment of the Indian National Congress in 1885
Objectives and demands of the Congress
student_activities: Prepare a short timeline of early Congress sessions
Discuss the demands made by early leaders

lesson_plan.title: Moderates and Extremists
topics_covered: Differences between moderate and extremist leaders
Contribution of leaders like Dadabhai Naoroji and Bal Gangadhar Tilak
student_activities: Create a comparison chart of moderate and extremist approaches

lesson_plan.title: Mass Participation in the National Movement
topics_covered: Role of peasants, workers and students
Spread of nationalist ideas across regions
teaching_strategy: Use of case examples from the textbook
student_activities: Identify groups that joined the freedom struggle
Discuss why mass participation was important

lesson_plan.title: Major National Movements
student_activities: Prepare a timeline of major movements
Write a short note on one movement

lesson_plan.title: Towards Independence
student_activities: Prepare key points about events before 1947

lesson_plan.title: Impact of the National Movement and Revision

teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze the strategies used by different leaders and movements. Use timelines and maps to help students understand how the national movement evolved over time across different parts of India.
